title: How to get a police certificate – South Africa - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – South Africa
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Police Clearance Certificate

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.

### If you live in South Africa
Apply in person at your local South African Police Service office. Check the South African Police Service website for full instructions.

### If you live outside of South Africa
You can apply at your nearest South African embassy or by mail to the South African Police Service office (check Contact information for the full address).

## Special considerations
Certificates from Ideco Biometrics are not valid for immigration purposes. Applicants should always request the standard Republic of South Africa police clearance certificate.

### Address for in-person applications
Criminal Record and Crime Scene Management South African Police Service Bothongo Plaza West CRC Client Service Centre 1st floor, room 14 271 Francis Baard Street Pretoria
Criminal Record and Crime Scene Management (For attention: Police Clearance Certificates) South Africa Police Service Private Bag X308 Pretoria South Africa 0001

### Email and telephone numbers
For more information about your Police Clearance Certificate, contact the South African Police Service.
Email: crc-nameclear@saps.gov.za
Telephone (South Africa): 012-393-3928 Telephone (international): 27 12 393 3928 Fax number (South Africa): 012-393-3909 Fax number (international): 27 12 393 3909
